title: Minimum Flips to Make a OR b Equal to c
hints: Consider the binary representation of a, b, and c. How does each bit position contribute independently to the condition a OR b == c?
For each bit position, compare the bits of a, b, and c. If the bit in c is 0, how many flips are needed for the corresponding bits in a and b?
If the bit in c is 1, check if at least one of the bits in a or b is already 1. If both are 0, one flip is required.
